speaker: GRUMIO
text: Fie, fie on all tired jades, on all mad masters,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: and all foul ways! Was ever man so beaten? Was

speaker: GRUMIO
text: ever man so ’rayed? Was ever man so weary? I am

speaker: GRUMIO
text: sent before to make a fire, and they are coming

speaker: GRUMIO
text: after to warm them. Now were not I a little pot and

speaker: GRUMIO
text: soon hot, my very lips might freeze to my teeth, my

speaker: GRUMIO
text: tongue to the roof of my mouth, my heart in my

speaker: GRUMIO
text: belly, ere I should come by a fire to thaw me. But I

speaker: GRUMIO
text: with blowing the fire shall warm myself. For, considering

speaker: GRUMIO
text: the weather, a taller man than I will take

speaker: GRUMIO
text: cold.—Holla, ho, Curtis!

speaker: CURTIS
text: Who is that calls so coldly?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: A piece of ice. If thou doubt it, thou mayst

speaker: GRUMIO
text: slide from my shoulder to my heel with no greater

speaker: GRUMIO
text: a run but my head and my neck. A fire, good Curtis!

speaker: CURTIS
text: Is my master and his wife coming, Grumio?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Oh, ay, Curtis, ay, and therefore fire, fire! Cast

speaker: GRUMIO
text: on no water.

speaker: CURTIS
text: Is she so hot a shrew as she’s reported?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: She was, good Curtis, before this frost. But

speaker: GRUMIO
text: thou know’st winter tames man, woman, and

speaker: GRUMIO
text: beast, for it hath tamed my old master and my new

speaker: GRUMIO
text: mistress and myself, fellow Curtis.

speaker: CURTIS
text: Away, you three-inch fool, I am no beast!

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Am I but three inches? Why, thy horn is a

speaker: GRUMIO
text: foot, and so long am I, at the least. But wilt thou

speaker: GRUMIO
text: make a fire? Or shall I complain on thee to our

speaker: GRUMIO
text: mistress, whose hand (she being now at hand) thou

speaker: GRUMIO
text: shalt soon feel, to thy cold comfort, for being slow in

speaker: GRUMIO
text: thy hot office?

speaker: CURTIS
text: I prithee, good Grumio, tell me, how goes the

speaker: CURTIS
text: world?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: A cold world, Curtis, in every office but thine,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: and therefore fire! Do thy duty, and have thy duty,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: for my master and mistress are almost frozen to

speaker: GRUMIO
text: death.

speaker: CURTIS
text: There’s fire ready. And therefore, good Grumio,

speaker: CURTIS
text: the news!

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Why, “Jack boy, ho boy!” and as much news

speaker: GRUMIO
text: as wilt thou.

speaker: CURTIS
text: Come, you are so full of cony-catching.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Why, therefore fire, for I have caught extreme

speaker: GRUMIO
text: cold. Where’s the cook? Is supper ready, the house

speaker: GRUMIO
text: trimmed, rushes strewed, cobwebs swept, the servingmen

speaker: GRUMIO
text: in their new fustian, their white stockings,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: and every officer his wedding garment on? Be

speaker: GRUMIO
text: the Jacks fair within, the Jills fair without, the

speaker: GRUMIO
text: carpets laid, and everything in order?

speaker: CURTIS
text: All ready. And therefore, I pray thee, news.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: First, know my horse is tired, my master and

speaker: GRUMIO
text: mistress fallen out.

speaker: CURTIS
text: How?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Out of their saddles into the dirt, and thereby

speaker: GRUMIO
text: hangs a tale.

speaker: CURTIS
text: Let’s ha’ t, good Grumio.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Lend thine ear.

speaker: CURTIS
text: Here.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: There!

speaker: CURTIS
text: This ’tis to feel a tale, not to hear a tale.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: And therefore ’tis called a sensible tale. And

speaker: GRUMIO
text: this cuff was but to knock at your ear and beseech

speaker: GRUMIO
text: list’ning. Now I begin: Imprimis, we came down a

speaker: GRUMIO
text: foul hill, my master riding behind my mistress—

speaker: CURTIS
text: Both of one horse?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: What’s that to thee?

speaker: CURTIS
text: Why, a horse.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Tell thou the tale! But hadst thou not crossed

speaker: GRUMIO
text: me, thou shouldst have heard how her horse fell,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: and she under her horse; thou shouldst have heard

speaker: GRUMIO
text: in how miry a place, how she was bemoiled, how he

speaker: GRUMIO
text: left her with the horse upon her, how he beat me

speaker: GRUMIO
text: because her horse stumbled, how she waded

speaker: GRUMIO
text: through the dirt to pluck him off me, how he swore,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: how she prayed that never prayed before, how I

speaker: GRUMIO
text: cried, how the horses ran away, how her bridle was

speaker: GRUMIO
text: burst, how I lost my crupper, with many things of

speaker: GRUMIO
text: worthy memory which now shall die in oblivion,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: and thou return unexperienced to thy grave.

speaker: CURTIS
text: By this reck’ning, he is more shrew than she.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Ay, and that thou and the proudest of you all

speaker: GRUMIO
text: shall find when he comes home. But what talk I of

speaker: GRUMIO
text: this? Call forth Nathaniel, Joseph, Nicholas, Phillip,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Walter, Sugarsop, and the rest. Let their heads

speaker: GRUMIO
text: be slickly combed, their blue coats brushed, and

speaker: GRUMIO
text: their garters of an indifferent knit. Let them curtsy

speaker: GRUMIO
text: with their left legs, and not presume to touch a hair

speaker: GRUMIO
text: of my master’s horse-tail till they kiss their hands.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Are they all ready?

speaker: CURTIS
text: They are.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Call them forth.

speaker: CURTIS
text: Do you hear, ho? You must meet

speaker: CURTIS
text: my master to countenance my mistress.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Why, she hath a face of her own.

speaker: CURTIS
text: Who knows not that?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Thou, it seems, that calls for company to

speaker: GRUMIO
text: countenance her.

speaker: CURTIS
text: I call them forth to credit her.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Why, she comes to borrow nothing of them.

speaker: NATHANIEL
text: Welcome home, Grumio.

speaker: PHILLIP
text: How now, Grumio?

speaker: JOSEPH
text: What, Grumio!

speaker: NICHOLAS
text: Fellow Grumio!

speaker: NATHANIEL
text: How now, old lad?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Welcome, you!—How now, you?—What,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: you!—Fellow, you!—And thus much for greeting.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Now, my spruce companions, is all ready and all

speaker: GRUMIO
text: things neat?

speaker: NATHANIEL
text: All things is ready. How near is our

speaker: NATHANIEL
text: master?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: E’en at hand, alighted by this. And therefore

speaker: GRUMIO
text: be not—Cock’s passion, silence! I hear my master.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Where be these knaves? What, no man at door

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: To hold my stirrup nor to take my horse?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Where is Nathaniel, Gregory, Phillip?

speaker:
ALL
               
              THE
               
              SERVANTS
text: Here! Here, sir, here, sir!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: “Here, sir! Here, sir! Here, sir! Here, sir!”

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: You loggerheaded and unpolished grooms.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: What? No attendance? No regard? No duty?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Where is the foolish knave I sent before?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Here, sir, as foolish as I was before.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: You peasant swain, you whoreson malt-horse drudge!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Did I not bid thee meet me in the park

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And bring along these rascal knaves with thee?

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Nathaniel’s coat, sir, was not fully made,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: And Gabriel’s pumps were all unpinked i’ th’ heel.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: There was no link to color Peter’s hat,

speaker: GRUMIO
text: And Walter’s dagger was not come from sheathing.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: There were none fine but Adam, Rafe, and Gregory.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: The rest were ragged, old, and beggarly.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Yet, as they are, here are they come to meet you.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Go, rascals, go, and fetch my supper in!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Where is the life that late I led?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Where are those—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Sit down, Kate, and welcome.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Soud, soud, soud, soud!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Why, when, I say?—Nay, good sweet Kate, be merry.—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Off with my boots, you rogues, you villains! When?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: It was the friar of orders gray,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: As he forth walkèd on his way—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Out, you rogue! You pluck my foot awry.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Take that!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And mend the plucking of the other.—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Be merry, Kate.—Some water here! What ho!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Where’s my spaniel Troilus? Sirrah, get you hence

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And bid my cousin Ferdinand come hither.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: One, Kate, that you must kiss and be acquainted with.—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Where are my slippers? Shall I have some water?—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Come, Kate, and wash, and welcome heartily.—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: You whoreson villain, will you let it fall?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Patience, I pray you, ’twas a fault unwilling.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: A whoreson beetle-headed flap-eared knave!—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Come, Kate, sit down. I know you have a stomach.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Will you give thanks, sweet Kate, or else shall I?—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: What’s this? Mutton?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: Ay.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Who brought it?

speaker: PETER
text: I.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: ’Tis burnt, and so is all the meat.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: What dogs are these? Where is the rascal cook?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: How durst you, villains, bring it from the dresser

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And serve it thus to me that love it not?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: There, take it to you, trenchers, cups, and all!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: You heedless joltheads and unmannered slaves!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: What, do you grumble? I’ll be with you straight.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I pray you, husband, be not so disquiet.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: The meat was well, if you were so contented.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I tell thee, Kate, ’twas burnt and dried away,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And I expressly am forbid to touch it,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: For it engenders choler, planteth anger,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And better ’twere that both of us did fast

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: (Since of ourselves, ourselves are choleric)

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Than feed it with such over-roasted flesh.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Be patient. Tomorrow ’t shall be mended,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And for this night we’ll fast for company.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Come, I will bring thee to thy bridal chamber.

speaker: NATHANIEL
text: Peter, didst ever see the like?

speaker: PETER
text: He kills her in her own humor.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Where is he?

speaker: CURTIS
text: In her chamber,

speaker: CURTIS
text: Making a sermon of continency to her,

speaker: CURTIS
text: And rails and swears and rates, that she (poor soul)

speaker: CURTIS
text: Knows not which way to stand, to look, to speak,

speaker: CURTIS
text: And sits as one new-risen from a dream.

speaker: CURTIS
text: Away, away, for he is coming hither!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Thus have I politicly begun my reign,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And ’tis my hope to end successfully.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: My falcon now is sharp and passing empty,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And, till she stoop, she must not be full-gorged,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: For then she never looks upon her lure.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Another way I have to man my haggard,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: To make her come and know her keeper’s call.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: That is, to watch her, as we watch these kites

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: That bate and beat and will not be obedient.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: She ate no meat today, nor none shall eat.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Last night she slept not, nor tonight she shall not.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: As with the meat, some undeservèd fault

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I’ll find about the making of the bed,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And here I’ll fling the pillow, there the bolster,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: This way the coverlet, another way the sheets.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Ay, and amid this hurly I intend

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: That all is done in reverend care of her.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And, in conclusion, she shall watch all night,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And, if she chance to nod, I’ll rail and brawl,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And with the clamor keep her still awake.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: This is a way to kill a wife with kindness.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And thus I’ll curb her mad and headstrong humor.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: He that knows better how to tame a shrew,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Now let him speak; ’tis charity to shew.
